title: 肉蒲團
author: Li, Yu
summary: Li Yu’s novel follows Unyangsheng, a clever young scholar consumed by erotic ambition, who seeks “the first beauty.” A solitary ascetic monk, Gufeng, warns him to renounce desire; Unyangsheng ignores counsel, uses divination to win Yuxiang, the strict iron‑door scholar’s sheltered daughter, and marries her. He exposes her to spring‑palace paintings and erotic manuals, transforming her innocence into ardor; the couple masters varied sexual techniques and relish voluptuous daily intimacy. The narrator frames lust as both nourishing and dangerous, using vivid erotic scenes to instruct readers about moral consequences, discipline, and the costs of unchecked desire. and social hypocrisy. too.

characters.name: 孤峰 (Gufeng / 皮布袋和尚)
characters.personality: ascetic, strict, principled, compassionate but pragmatic
characters.lifespan: Remains a solitary ascetic who refuses easy discipleship; warns Unyangsheng and composes a verse as admonition within the excerpt.

characters.name: 未央生 (Unyangsheng)
characters.personality: handsome, talented, clever, lustful, ambitious for fame and a perfect lover
characters.lifespan: Pursues erotic desire; ignores monk's counsel, marries Yuxiang, and actively instructs and awakens her sexually within the excerpt.

characters.name: 玉香 (Yuxiang)
characters.personality: initially sheltered, modest and virtuous, later curious and sexually responsive
characters.lifespan: Sheltered daughter of the iron‑door scholar; after marriage she is introduced to erotic books and becomes ardent and skilled in intimacy in the excerpt.

characters.name: 鐵扉道人 (the iron‑door scholar / Yuxiang's father)
characters.lifespan: Maintains rigorous household discipline and refuses casual meetings; within the excerpt he remains a stern guardian whose daughter is later married off.

characters.name: 媒婆 (the matchmaker)
characters.lifespan: Acts as intermediary; arranges and reports on the betrothal process to Unyangsheng and the iron‑door scholar in the excerpt.

characters.name: 仙欒 / 大仙 (the diviner who writes poems)
characters.lifespan: Performs poetic divination for Unyangsheng; supplies prophetic verses that influence the marriage decision in the excerpt.

characters.name: 回道人 / 呂純陽 (the Reclusive Taoist cited)
characters.lifespan: Referenced authoritatively by an oracle poem in the excerpt; not an active participant.

characters.name: 朋友 (the friend who brings the diviner)
characters.lifespan: Brings the diviner/仙欒 to Unyangsheng's house to consult about the courtship in the excerpt.

characters.name: 家童 / 伴當 (house servants / attendants)
characters.lifespan: Serve Unyangsheng and perform errands such as summoning matchmakers and guests in the excerpt.

characters.name: 孟子 (Mencius, referenced)
characters.personality: quoted as exemplar of moral persuasion
characters.lifespan: Appears as a quoted precedent in the monk's moral argument; not an active character in the plot excerpt.

characters.name: 齊宣王 (King Xuan of Qi, referenced)
characters.personality: quoted as an example of appetitive ruler
characters.lifespan: Mentioned in moral and rhetorical passages by the narrator and monk; not active in narrative events.

characters.name: 太王 / 姜女 (referenced historical figures)
characters.lifespan: Cited in argument and analogy by characters in the excerpt; no direct role.

locations.name: 處州 (historic Chuzhou, modern Lishui, Zhejiang)
